response: solution The primary error in the erroneous code is likely a failure to handle duplicate elements by interleaving them, causing adjacent equal values when the array contains many duplicates. solution A robust approach involves sorting the array and then filling the result by placing smaller elements in even indices and larger elements in odd indices, but this must be done carefully to avoid adjacent duplicates. solution To achieve O(n) time without full sorting, we can use a modified quickselect to find the median, partition the array into elements less than or equal to the median and elements greater than or equal to the median, and then interleave them using a virtual index mapping to prevent adjacent collisions. solution This virtual index mapping transforms the actual index i to a mapped index (1 + 2*i) % (n | 1), ensuring that elements from the same half of the partition are never placed next to each other. solution The final solution handles both positive and negative integers correctly because the comparison logic relies only on relative ordering, not on the sign of the numbers.
